data.title: 10 overbought stocks to watch as Wall Street sinks, signaling potential pullbacks
data.description:
Wall Street began the trading week on a negative note, with major market indices showing a decline as selling pressure quickly emerged, following last week’s downturn. Despite the broader market retreating, certain individual stocks have continued to rise, pushing them into overbought territory, as indicated by their Relative Strength Index technical indicator.

This raises an important question: Are these moves into overbought conditions a signal of potential exhaustion, suggesting the possibility of short-term pullbacks, or do these stocks still have room to run? 

Seeking Alpha has identified the top ten most overbought stocks, providing valuable insights into potential market corrections. With these stocks showing RSI levels above 70 in a generally bearish market, investors may want to keep a close eye on potential price tops and consider the risk of further declines. Screened names include U.S. stocks with market caps north of $10B. List is ranked by RSI. 

No. 10: Amgen (AMGN), RSI of 74.

No. 9: Brown & Brown (BRO), RSI of 74.

No. 8: American Water Works Company (AWK), RSI of 76.

No. 7: LKQ Corporation (LKQ), RSI of 76.

No. 6: Expeditors International of Washington (EXPD), RSI of 76.

No. 5: AbbVie (ABBV), RSI of 76.

No. 4: Verizon Communications (VZ), RSI of 77. 

No. 3: Gilead Sciences (GILD), RSI of 78.

No. 2: Yum! Brands (YUM), RSI of 79. 

No. 1: Intra-Cellular Therapies (ITCI), RSI of 85.

data.title: KeyCorp upgraded to Buy at Citi on solid fundamentals, cheap valuation after sector selloff
data.description:
Citi upgraded KeyCorp (NYSE:KEY) to Buy from Neutral as the sector's recent selloff offers an attractive entry point for KEY, which analyst Keith Horowitz ranks as "among the best value on our implied cost of equity valuation screen."

The stock, with solid fundamentals, appears to trade cheap at 8x normalized earnings, he added.

"With credit concerns mounting amidst recently elevated concerns for a weakening macro backdrop, we believe this name should be a safe haven with below average credit risk (limited consumer and CRE exposure) and very strong 9.8% pro forma CET1 ratio," Horowitz wrote in a note to clients.

Citi trimmed its 2025 EPS estimate for KeyCorp (NYSE:KEY) to $1.50 from $1.55 on the dimmer near-term outlook, increases its 2026 EPS estimate to $1.80 from $1.75 and its 2027 EPS estimate to $2.00 from $1.95, on revised assumptions on expenses.

Also, the Key (KEY) management plans to ask the board to reinstate its stock buyback authorization, which could also support the stock, Citi said.

The Buy rating contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Hold and agrees with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

KeyCorp (KEY) stock dipped 2.0% in early Monday trading amid a risk-off market backdrop.

data.title: Credo dips despite upgrade at Susquehanna on growing portfolio of AI connectivity tech
data.description:
Susquehanna upgraded Credo Technology (NASDAQ:CRDO) to Positive from Neutral noting that the company has a growing portfolio of key AI connectivity technologies that are underappreciated.

The firm reiterated its $60 price target on the shares of Credo, which provides connectivity solutions.

Shares of Credo fell about 6% on Monday. 

Analysts led by Christopher Rolland said on paper, January (quarter) results were fantastic, with strong follow-through into the April guidance, but customer concentration (Amazon 86% of total) spooked investors.

Credo had derived 86% of its revenue from Amazon Web Services (AMZN) during the third quarter.

"While we don’t expect Amazon to sustain at >$100 million quarterly, we also don’t expect the company’s other customers to sustain at near-zero contribution," said the analysts.

The analysts noted that in the near-doubling of revenue sequentially, Credo faced supply limitations and favored Amazon over others in its allocation, but the analysts expect this dynamic to normalize starting April and beyond.

Despite near-term market skepticism around AI buildouts, the analysts said, they are leaning into Credo’s interconnect portfolio for several reasons.

The analysts said that, with Active Copper Cables, or ACCs, sidelined for Nvidia's GB300, Active Electrical Cables, or AECs, offer the most affordable next-best alternative to passive copper (for solutions under seven meters); In addition, as clusters expand from 10,000 to 100,000 to 1 million GPU/XPU clusters, AECs hold signal integrity and reliability advantages over laser-based optics as these interconnects become exponentially more valuable as one failure can undermine a multi-million-dollar training run.

The analysts added that Credo’s advanced Digital Signal Processors, or DSP, solution is transferable to optical transceivers. In addition, greenfield Peripheral Component Interconnect Express, or PCIe, retimer opportunity is showing long-term promise (more than $1B total addressable market, or TAM in 2027) as well as PCIe AEC cable opportunity, as the company taps into the emerging scale-up opportunity, according to the analysts.

Rolland and his team said that this growing portfolio of the most important interconnect technologies makes Credo an interesting take-out candidate at nearly $7B enterprise value, or EV.

data.title: Gold Reserve makes stalking horse bid for Citgo parent shares
data.description:
Gold Reserve (OTCQX:GDRZF) said Monday its Dalinar Energy subsidiary submitted an offer that could be chosen as the starting bid in a court-organized auction of shares in Venezuela-owned refiner Citgo Petroleum's parent PDV Holding.

The U.S. District Court for the District of Delaware, which is handling the auction for PDV Holding, is expected to pick the offer as a "stalking horse" bid, which is the starting point for the minimally accepted offer.

If selected, a topping-off period would begin for the court to receive rival bids, with a final hearing on the sale set for July, Gold Reserve (OTCQX:GDRZF) said, adding that the terms of the bid would remain confidential until all offers have been reviewed.

The Delaware court last year found PDV Holding liable for Citgo's debt defaults and expropriation after Venezuela's President Chavez' nationalization wave and President Maduro's failed debt payments.

A total of 18 creditors holding arbitration rulings and court cases against Venezuela and state company PDVSA totaling $21.3B are pursuing proceeds from the auction of shares that was launched in October 2023, but a first bidding round last year failed to meet the creditors' expectations.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson met main goal in its oral treatment trial for ulcerative colitis
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) said on Monday that its mid-stage study of icotrokinra, for the treatment of moderate to severe active ulcerative colitis, met main goal.

The trial showed positive clinical responses across all tested doses of icotrokinra, the company's targeted oral peptide that selectively blocks the IL-23 receptor, in adults with moderately to severely active ulcerative colitis. It also met key secondary goals, including clinical remission, symptom reduction, and signs of endoscopic improvement.

At Week 12, 63.5% of patients on the highest dose of icotrokinra showed improvement, compared to 27% of those on a placebo. Additionally, 30.2% of patients on the highest dose reached clinical remission, versus 11.1% in the placebo group, JNJ said.

The company  added that Icotrokinra was well tolerated, with similar rates of side effects reported in both the treatment and placebo groups.

data.title: Enstar launches cash tender offer for junior subordinated notes due 2040
data.description:
• Insurance and reinsurance company Enstar Group (NASDAQ:ESGR) announced on Monday a cash tender offer for its outstanding 5.750% fixed-rate reset junior subordinated notes due 2040.
  • ESGR shares were trading +0.04% pre-market at $331.97.
  • The notes were issued by the unit Enstar Finance LLC.
  • The junior subordinated notes had a principal amount outstanding of $350M, the March 10 statement by the company said.
  • The consideration paid for the notes that are validly tendered and accepted for purchase will be $1,000 per $1,000 principal amount of notes.
  • The tender offer launches today, and is expected to expire on March 14, 5 PM New York City time.
  • Enstar expects the settlement date to be the third business day after the expiration time, which comes to March 19.

data.title: Genie energy targets $40M-$50M adjusted EBITDA in 2025 amid strategic growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Genie Energy (GNE) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Michael Stein, CEO, highlighted a solid Q4 2024 performance across both retail and renewables businesses, with the company achieving the high end of adjusted EBITDA guidance for the year. GRE expanded its customer base by 23,000 net new meters in Q4 and over 60,000 for the full year, marking a 17% growth. The company plans to continue building its meter book in 2025, particularly emphasizing growth in the Texas and California markets.
  • CEO Stein emphasized the progress in GREW, which reported a 120% increase in gross profit compared to 2023, driven by improved SG&A efficiencies and operational performance. The Roded venture began generating its first revenues and aims to scale operations in Israel.
  • CFO Avi Goldin pointed out that consolidated revenue for Q4 2024 was $102.9 million, with a slight year-over-year decrease of 1.9% due to seasonal factors. GRE accounted for $98.4 million, with electricity sales representing 83.5% of GRE’s revenue.
  • Goldin detailed that consolidated gross profit was $33.5 million in Q4, maintaining a gross margin of 32.5%, while adjusted EBITDA decreased slightly to $11.1 million. GREW’s gross profit increased by 38% year-over-year, reaching $1.5 million.

Outlook

  • Genie Energy expects annual consolidated adjusted EBITDA guidance for 2025 to remain between $40 million and $50 million. The company also plans to grow its cash reserves, continue stock buybacks, and invest in growth initiatives across both established and new businesses.
  • Management anticipates significant developments in the renewables segment, including the completion and commissioning of a community solar project in 2025, with plans to begin construction on two to three additional projects during the year.

Financial Results

  • For Q4 2024, consolidated revenue was $102.9 million, a 1.9% decrease from the previous year. The retail energy segment (GRE) remained stable at $98.4 million, while renewables (GREW) posted a 30.1% revenue decrease to $4.5 million.
  • Consolidated gross profit was $33.5 million, with a gross margin up by 40 basis points to 32.5%. GRE’s gross profit decreased slightly by 1.8% to $31.9 million, driven by compressed electricity sales margins, partially offset by improved gas sales margins.
  • Consolidated loss from operations decreased 39.2% year-over-year to $20.8 million, primarily due to lower non-cash insurance loss reserves.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 2024 was $11.1 million, down 2.8% from the prior year, mainly reflecting investments in meter acquisitions and reduced electricity margins.
  • For the full year 2024, consolidated revenue was $425.2 million, with adjusted EBITDA at $48.5 million, reaching the high end of guidance.

Q&A

  • NOT_APPLICABLE: The Q&A session was not included as no questions were posed during the conference call.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts did not express concerns or pose questions during the call, leading to an absence of discernible sentiment from their side.
  • Management's tone was consistently positive, with confidence in achieving growth targets. CEO Stein praised the team’s efforts in 2024 and expressed optimism for 2025, particularly highlighting growth opportunities in new and existing markets.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a similar tone of confidence, with an emphasis on operational improvements and strategic investments.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 2024 guidance language remained consistent with Q3, maintaining adjusted EBITDA targets and emphasizing strategic investments.
  • Key metric variations include a slight revenue decrease in Q4, driven by seasonal consumption changes, whereas Q3 saw a higher contribution from Texas market expansions.
  • Analysts' focus remained unobserved in Q4 as no questions were posed, contrasting with potential engagement in prior quarters.

Risks and Concerns

  • Seasonal consumption fluctuations impacted quarterly revenue and margins. Management acknowledged lower per-meter electricity and gas consumption in Q4 due to mild weather.
  • Margin compression on electricity sales was noted as a continued challenge, partially mitigated by fixed-price meter strategies and improved gas sales margins.
  • Investment risks include scaling operations in the Roded venture and expanding renewable energy projects, which require capital allocation and operational efficiency.

Final Takeaway

Genie Energy closed 2024 with strong financial and operational results, achieving the high end of adjusted EBITDA guidance and demonstrating solid growth in both retail and renewables. Management remains optimistic for 2025, targeting $40 million to $50 million in adjusted EBITDA while pursuing expansion in Texas and California, scaling renewable projects, and leveraging strategic investments to boost long-term shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Nasdaq drops 2%, S&P, and Dow fall as well as concerns about the economy grow
data.description:
Wall Street opened sharply lower on Monday as worries about the health of the economy cast a cloud of concern among investors after Donald Trump's interview with Fox News. 

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was -1.6%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -2.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -1.5%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 5 basis points to 4.24%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 5 basis points to 3.96%.

The fears of a potential recession in the U.S. grew after Trump was asked if he was expecting a recession this year in the interview. “I hate to predict things like that,” he said. “There is a period of transition because what we’re doing is very big. We’re bringing wealth back to America. That’s a big thing.” 

"Trump also failed to rule out a recession. After the 2024 soft landing, markets are getting more concerned about economic growth risks. As the pandemic illustrated, supply chain disruption can  create growth problems. Disruption to trade and government both raise risks," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Furthermore, adding to the bearish sentiment, China’s return to deflation has fueled worries about global growth prospects.

China's consumer prices fell 0.7% year-on-year in February, a 13-month low, exceeding the expected 0.5% drop and reversing January’s 0.5% rise.

Wall Street's major averages closed higher Friday, aided by Fed Chairman Jerome Powell's economic reassurance, but stocks sank for the week as investors wrestled with tariff twists from the Trump administration.

"Looking back, it was a truly seismic week for markets last week, as investors grappled with historic fiscal news out of Germany, European rearmament proposals, fresh tariff developments, and a U.S. jobs report on Friday," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

But even as investors were enthusiastic about the fiscal news, the tariff developments led to a broader risk-off move that hit global equity markets. In fact, the S&P 500 posted its biggest weekly decline in six months, Reid said.

The economic calendar is light on Monday, with mainly the NY Fed 1-Year consumer inflation expectations on tap.

"The New York Fed’s survey of inflation expectations is due—not reliable as a prediction, but perhaps signaling whether consumers are concerned," Donovan said.

data.title: Apple 2025 iPhone shipment estimate lowered by Citi on Siri upgrade delay
data.description:
Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) 2025 calendar year iPhone shipment estimate was lowered by Citi Research due to a delay in its upgrade to Siri, the digital assistant that uses voice commands to perform tasks.

"The expectation has been that the big update of Siri with on-screen awareness, personal context and deep app integration will be released sometime this April/May, but Apple confirmed on March 7 that the company sees delay in these features and now expects to roll it out in the coming year, which we view as a negative as it would have been a catalyst to drive up higher refresh this year," said Citi analysts, led by Atif Malik, in a Monday investor note.

Citi lowered its 2025 iPhone shipments to 232M, which would be a year-over-year increase of 2% versus its previous estimate of 5% growth. It maintained its 5% year-over-year gain to 244M units for calendar year 2026.

Still, the financial firm contends Apple is finding inroads in introducing Apple Intelligence features in China.

"Apple has not confirmed a date for Apple Intelligence rollout in China as the company is still working through regulatory processes, but we view the confirmation of partnerships with Alibaba/Baidu and expected roll out of additional languages in April including simplified Chinese as a positive step," Malik noted.

The U.S. tech giant has several teams in China and the U.S. working to adapt its AI service Apple Intelligence for the region. Besides the engineering work, Apple still faces regulatory hurdles in China.

Apple continues to work with Chinese company Baidu (BIDU) to develop AI features for iPhones in China even after bringing in another local partner, Alibaba (BABA).
